$comment: EXAMPLE — filled instance, not a template. The machine-readable half of example/governance/13-domain-hard-lines.md for Snip (a fictional URL shortener). One entry per hard line, ids HL-1..3 ↔ the '### 1..3 —' headings, decisions D-10..12 traced. Imitate the shape; don't edit these to build a real project. Verify: node enforcement/scripts/check-hard-lines.mjs --self-test --manifest example/enforcement/hard-lines.json

hardLines.$comment: The 'files' scope is the teaching point: request-path code only (^src/), so migrations and seed scripts — where the service role legitimately appears — never trip it.
hardLines.name: Tenant isolation

patterns.message: service-role key in request-path code bypasses RLS — user requests run under the caller's JWT only (HL-1, D-10)
shouldNotMatch: const db = createClient(url, anonKey, { global: { headers: { Authorization: jwt } } })

patterns.message: hard-delete on links frees a short_code for reuse — soft-delete + tombstone instead (HL-2, D-11)
shouldMatch: await db.query('DELETE FROM links WHERE id = $1', [id])
shouldNotMatch: await db.query('UPDATE links SET deleted_at = now() WHERE id = $1', [id])

patterns.message: short_code is immutable — never reassigned after issue (HL-2, D-11)
shouldMatch: UPDATE links SET short_code = 'abc123' WHERE id = $1
shouldNotMatch: UPDATE links SET title = $2 WHERE id = $1

patterns.message: click_events is append-only — corrections insert a new row, never mutate (HL-3, D-12)
no mutable click_count — counts are COUNT(*) over the click_events ledger (HL-3, D-12)
